english: 1. = gegs (gegs), hindrance, obstruction, seldom. - 2. an evil spirit, demon, devil, like gdoṅ (gdong); bgegs-kyi rgyál-po (bgegs kyi rgyal po) bi-nā-ya-ka Mil. frq. (Ssk. विनायक a remover, of obstacles; the god Gaṇesha etc.).

I. vb. 1. to put on clothes etc., pf., imp. bgos; lham rtág-tu bgos (lham rtag tu bgos) always wear shoes S.g.; esp. to put on armour. - 2. v. under bgod-pa (bgod pa). II. sbst. clothes, clothing, bgó-ba daṅ bzá-ba (bgo ba dang bza' ba) food and clothes Dzl.

(bgog-pá (bgog pa) Sch. is perh. a provincialism) pf., imp. bgos, fut. bgo; W. inf. *gó-ce*; imp. *gos tói* to divide, nor (nor) an inheritance; to divide in ciphering, graṅs (grangs) a number; to distribute, śas-śás-su (shas shas su) into shares, mí-rnams-la (mi rnams la) to or amongst people Dzl. Comp. bgod-byéd (bgod byed) divisor Wdk., and accordingly also bgo-byá (bgo bya) dividend. - bgo-skál (bgo skal) 1. share, lot, B. and col. 2. the doctrine of strict retribution Thgr. frq. - *gó-k̇ań* W. sharer, partaker, heir, joint-heir, - bgo-bśá (bgo bsha') = bgo-skál (bgo skal), bgo-bśá byéd-pa (bgo bsha' byed pa) to distribute, allot, apportion, nor (nor) the property Thgy., la (la) among Stg.

pf. bgoms Sch., to walk, to step, to stride, góm-pa bgóm-pa (gom pa bgom pa) Lex. to make steps; tém pa-la bgóm-pa (them pa la bgom pa) to step over the threshold; bgom gró-ba (bgom gro ba) to pace, to walk slowly; bgoms túb-pa (bgoms thub pa) to begin to walk (?) Sch.
acc. to Zam. = brgyáṅ-ba (brgyang ba), v. rgyoṅ-ba (rgyong ba).
eleg. for byá-ba (bya ba), 1. fut. of bgyíd-pa (bgyid pa). - 2. sbst. action, deed.

pf. bgyis, fut. bgyi, imp. gyis, eleg. for byéd-pa (byed pa) 1. to make, to manufacture; gyis zér-bai gzúgs (gyis zer ba'i gzugs) the images regarding to which there had been said: 'make them!' i.e. the bespoken, ordered images Glr.; to do, to act, to perform, las bgyíd-pa (las bgyid pa) to do a work, bká bźin-du bgyio (bka' bzhin du bgyio) according to the word will be acted Dzl.; nye-gnás bgyíd-pa (nye gnas bgyid pa) to act the disciple = to be a disciple Dzl.; mi-la gnód-pa bgyis (mi la gnod pa bgyis) I have hurt the man, I have done him harm Dzl.; bu yód-par gyis śig (bu yod par gyis shig) make, bring it about, that a child be (born)! Dzl.; rgyál-bu ma śór-ba gyis śig (rgyal bu ma shor ba gyis shig) see that you do not let the prince escape Pth. (ba (ba) for bar (bar) in the more careless popular style). - 2. to say, źes bgyis (zhes bgyis) so he said Dzl.; źes bgyi-ba (zhes bgyi ba) the so called Dzl.

pf. bgraṅs, to number, count, calculate bsód-nams-kyi tshad (bsod nams kyi tshad) the amount of merits Glr.; bgraṅ-bya (bgrang bya) what may be numbered, numerable; bgráṅ-bar mi byá-ba (bgrang bar mi bya ba), bgraṅ-du méd-pa (bgrang du med pa), bgraṅ-yás (bgrang yas) innumerable; bgraṅ-ṗréṅ (bgrang phreng) rosary, beads Glr., also the garland of human skulls, often seen as an attribute of terrible deities.

1. to open wide, mig bgrád-pa (mig bgrad pa) to stare, goggle, ka bgrád-pa (kha bgrad pa) to gape Glr., Cs.; rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) to part the legs wide, to straddle, cf. bsgrád-pa (bsgrad pa). - 2. to scratch Sch. (spelled more corr. brád-pa (brad pa)).
pf. bgruṅs to cause to deposit, to strain, to depurate Cs., e.g. rnyóg-ma (rnyog ma) impure water Lex..
pf. bgrus, fut. bgru, to clear from the husks, to husk, to shell, bgrús-pai bras (bgrus pa'i 'bras) Lex. husked rice.

pf. bgrés, resp. to grow old, often with an additional sku-nas (sku nas) in years (v. na (na)) Dzl.; bgres-rgyúd (bgres rgyud) weakness of old age, infirmity Pth.: bgrés-po (bgres po), in W. pronounced *ré(s)-po*, an old man, a man gray with age, hoary; *ré(s)-mo* fem.
occasionally for 1. sgréṅ-ba (sgreng ba). 2. bgráṅ-ba (bgrang ba).

pf. bgros (resp. bka-bgrós mdzád-pa (bka' bgros mdzad pa) Pth.) to argue, discuss, deliberate, consider; the subject discussed is gen. a direct quotation: ći pyir di-ltar gyur ċes bgrós-nas (ci phyir 'di ltar gyur ces bgros nas) to converse on the cause of the present state of things Dzl.; źes p̌an- (zhes phan -) <
